title: Helosciadium oppositifolium
taxonomy: Helosciadium oppositifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It belongs to the genus Helosciadium. The genus Helosciadium is further classified under the family Apiaceae and the order Apiales. Helosciadium oppositifolium is native to Australia and New Zealand, and it is a perennial plant. Further classification of the species within the kingdom and its exact rank within the family and genus is not specified.
uses: Helosciadium oppositifolium, also known as Scottish parsley, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits.
